@DarkEndMoon1 @mrddmia @RonColeman I'm not saying that wouldn't be a good rule to have. I am saying that the claim that it's obvious that it's the case seems quite absurd without some citation to some authority holding so.

@DarkEndMoon1 @mrddmia @RonColeman It is not obvious to me that given that Congress has the power to create or destroy courts inferior to the Supreme Court, they nevertheless don't have the power to monitor with little or no restriction what goes on in those inferior courts.

@dogg_not @RAHarrisonPA @RobertFreundLaw @DrSarteschi Also, to address the libel issue more directly, we don't hold people liable for libel absent some evidence of wrongdoing. If, with no wrongdoing, I libel you and cause you severe damages, you still have no remedy at law in the United States.

Why do we do this? Because we don't
@mrddmia @RonColeman Do you have any citation to precedential holdings that make it obvious that they couldn't do so even if a statute says they can? It's certainly not obvious to me.

It is likely no less important as a form of speech as television and the internet.

probability.last_reasoning: Bear case increases from 10% to 12%: extreme fear (F&G 14), compound stress deteriorating, active geopolitical escalation (US-Spain trade cutoff, Hormuz still live), PermissionedDEX zero adoption at 11 days, Ondo competing pathway gaining traction. Base case increases from 40% to 42%: Ripple Payments expansion is substantive but market conditions suppress near-term upside. Mid case decreases from 38% to 34%: Geopolitical headwinds and macro stress make the mid-case scenario harder to reach by EOY unless stress resolves. Bull case holds at 12%: paradoxically, the thesis validation scenario (Japan stress cascade → settlement infrastructure demand) is becoming MORE plausible as compound stress pre-loads, but the path to $20+ requires crisis resolution AND adoption acceleration simultaneously.

bear_case.score_reasoning: Elevated from prior assessment. Three factors driving increased counter-thesis pressure: (1) PermissionedDEX at zero adoption after 11 days while Ondo/Binance/ADGM builds a competing tokenization pathway, (2) RLUSD only 22% on XRPL ($345M of $1.584B) — the rest on Ethereum/custodial, undermining XRPL-specific thesis, (3) extreme fear environment (F&G 14) with no ETF flow data to assess institutional conviction.

competing_infrastructure.name: SWIFT GPI
competing_infrastructure.status_text: No new competitive signals. SWIFT's blockchain pilot data remains thin. Ripple's $100B+ processed claim approaches SWIFT's daily volume narrative but cumulative vs. daily comparison is misleading.

competing_infrastructure.name: Visa B2B Connect
competing_infrastructure.status_text: Visa/Stripe stablecoin card expansion to 100+ countries is a major competitive move. This builds stablecoin payment infrastructure at scale without XRPL. Visa's distribution advantage is formidable.

competing_infrastructure.name: JPMorgan Kinexys
competing_infrastructure.status_text: Dimon's comments on stablecoin regulation suggest JPMorgan positioning to benefit from bank-favorable stablecoin rules. Kinexys would be advantaged if yield-bearing stablecoins face bank-level regulation.

competing_infrastructure.name: BIS Project Nexus
competing_infrastructure.status_text: No new signals. DTCC's 'trusted, neutral foundation' positioning suggests FMIs are staking claims in digital settlement without committing to blockchain-native solutions.

competing_infrastructure.name: Ethereum Institutional
competing_infrastructure.status_text: Ondo tokenized stocks approved on Binance/ADGM framework — this is institutional tokenization gaining regulatory approval on a non-XRPL pathway. ~$1.24B of RLUSD sits on Ethereum, not XRPL. Ethereum is capturing institutional stablecoin and RWA flows that the XRPL thesis requires.

bear_case.odl_stagnation: ODL volume remains completely opaque. Ripple's $100B+ cumulative processed claim cannot be verified or decomposed into ODL-specific flows. The decision to stop publishing ODL data is itself a bear signal — transparent growth would be marketed.
bear_case.token_velocity_concern: No token velocity data available. XRP 24h volume at $3.25B against $83.5B market cap yields a V/MC ratio of ~3.9% — moderate but not indicative of high-velocity institutional settlement usage. Without ODL decomposition, cannot distinguish speculative trading from utility flows.

macro_headwinds.name: Global Recession Risk
macro_headwinds.status_text: US-Spain trade cutoff, Iran escalation, extreme fear (F&G 14), and compound stress pre-loading across all three legs point to rising recession probability. DXY collapse to 99.23 reflects confidence erosion.

macro_headwinds.name: Crypto Winter Signals
macro_headwinds.status_text: F&G at 14 is extreme fear territory. XRP at $1.37 is down significantly from recent highs. CoinDesk 20 down 3.3%. However, institutional infrastructure continues building (Ripple Payments, CFTC perp futures) which distinguishes from prior crypto winters.

macro_headwinds.name: Regulatory Reversal Risk
macro_headwinds.status_text: Regulatory trajectory remains favorable — CFTC perp futures approval within 30 days, 7 spot XRP ETFs live. No reversal signals. Dimon's stablecoin-as-banks comment is the only potential headwind.

macro_headwinds.name: Rate Hike Extension
macro_headwinds.status_text: US 10Y at 3.97% (stale Feb 27 data). DXY collapse to 99.23 suggests market pricing rate cuts, not hikes. BOJ is the rate hike risk — trapped into defending yen via tightening, which could cascade through JGB markets.

bear_case.bear_narrative: INCREASE MONITORING. Compound stress is pre-loaded and approaching — all three legs deteriorating simultaneously with USD/JPY already at the 157 elevated threshold, JGB 17bps from breach, Brent trending toward $85. Two kill switches escalated to WARNING: ODL volume opacity is now classified as SUSPICIOUS_ABSENCE after behavioral analysis confirms Ripple is deliberately withholding while diversifying away from ODL dependence, and the Q3 2026 transparency deadline is the meta-kill-switch that determines whether this thesis remains falsifiable at all. The thesis paradox is live: the same geopolitical fragmentation and macro stress that threatens XRP's price in the near term is precisely the environment the settlement thesis was designed to address. Bear pressure at 38 is moderate — institutional deployment continues (Ripple March 3 platform expansion, seven live ETFs, SBI deepening), but the core settlement throughput claim cannot be verified, and we are monitoring a thesis whose most important metric is invisible.
